title: Forgotten Depths - A top 10 contender (solo dungeon crawl)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FG5G2G-XjhE

Daniel (@dungeondive) returns to Forgotten Depths with the second edition, declaring this one of the most novel and unique dungeon crawls ever made. The game puts you in control of three heroes—Bren the fighter, Sil the wizard, and Ava the rogue—exploring three interconnected ecologies with a smaller, better-organized rulebook and refined box size. The genius lies in five distinct innovations that set Forgotten Depths apart: tile placement creates meaningful spatial puzzles where you choose how to navigate and encounter enemies; hero-specific treasure decks ensure your wizard finds spells and your fighter finds weapons; combat offers genuine choice through card selection with push-your-luck tension; Marin Churchland's soft, watercolor-style art creates a dreamlike, utterly unique visual identity unlike any other game; and three exceptional expansions add narrative depth, sub-dungeons, and even a meta-layer with portals between ecologies.

The Camp Dialogue expansion focuses purely on character conversations during rest stops, deepening your connection to this world's history. The Vault of Yar introduces both lore cards and a challenging sub-dungeon, while the Ever Chamber adds chimeric monsters and a completely alternate way to win. Daniel, after a week of constant play, genuinely believes Forgotten Depths has an excellent chance of entering his personal top 10 list.

----

What makes Forgotten Depths' combination of tight mechanics, spectacular art, and bold novelty feel genuinely different from other contemporary dungeon crawls you've experienced?
